mechanism_narrative: Persistent elevation in the unemployment rate signals sustained labor market slack, reducing household income and consumer spending, which depresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If over any rolling 2-year window, month-over-month increases in UNRATE are associated with positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the rank information coefficient of UNRATE change on forward returns is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5%, it reflects the Federal Reserve's aggressive easing in response to severe economic distress, signaling that the crisis is acute and further deterioration is likely, which drives negative near-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If in all episodes with FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, the average 20-day forward return is positive, or the percentage of negative forward returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and payroll employment indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and labor income, with reinforcing feedback that deepens recession expectations and produces strongly negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both series declining month-over-month) does not yield negative 20-day forward returns in at least 65% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a linear model is not significantly negative at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: During deep recessions where year-over-year real GDP contraction exceeds 2%, a further decline in GDPC1 signals intensifying output collapse, directly impairing corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If during periods flagged as deep recession, occasions of negative quarter-over-quarter GDP growth are not followed by negative 20-day forward returns more often than by positive returns (binomial test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production is a coincident indicator of real output; its sustained decline signals shrinking manufacturing activity and corporate earnings, which consistently depresses equity prices over short horizons of one week.
failure_condition: If the correlation between month-over-month changes in INDPRO and 5-day S&P 500 forward returns is positive over any continuous 5-year period, or the signal fails to achieve a negative mean return during INDPRO decline months.

mechanism_narrative: Deflation in consumer prices, measured by a negative year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL, signals acute demand deficiency and raises the risk of a debt-deflation spiral, which is strongly detrimental to equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: If during months with year-over-year consumer price deflation, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is positive, or the proportion of negative returns is statistically indistinguishable from 50% (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts combined with falling payroll employment captures simultaneous deterioration in residential investment and labor market health, which together have historically preceded deep equity drawdowns on a quarterly horizon by amplifying recession signals.
failure_condition: If the joint decline signal (both series falling month-over-month) does not predict negative 60-day forward returns with accuracy exceeding 60%, or if the rank information coefficient of the joint signal is not significantly negative at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: In the initial phase of a post-recession recovery, an inflection in industrial production from a prolonged trough signals improving manufacturing activity, which leads investors to anticipate earnings growth and produces positive equity returns over the following quarter.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (INDPRO trough followed by at least 2 months of increase)
failure_condition: If during regimes identified as post-recession recovery, months with increasing INDPRO are not followed by positive 60-day returns at least 60% of the time, or the average return during those periods does not exceed the unconditional average at the 5% significance level.
